title: Make Behavior Predictable and Feedback Useful
summary: Build and verify design consistency feedback in survey control and status consistency review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain design consistency feedback from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply design consistency feedback without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue the Community Needs Survey redesign here. -->
starterFiles.css: /* Continue the survey design system here. */

options.text: Survey control and status consistency review case 1: consistency means every component must look identical regardless of purpose.
Survey control and status consistency review case 1: standardize patterns by purpose and state, preserve meaningful distinctions, and verify immediate feedback plus recovery across the complete task.
Survey control and status consistency review case 1: give every message the same blue treatment so users learn one universal state color.

steps.title: 04 · Read the survey control and status consistency review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which claim preserves design consistency feedback when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: survey control and status consistency review.
steps.why: Make Behavior Predictable and Feedback Useful makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in survey control and status consistency review.

content.text: Consistency means comparable controls and states follow predictable patterns, while different purposes remain distinguishable. Feedback should be timely, specific, visible, programmatically exposed, and proportional to the action. Hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, empty, and success states need a shared behavioral language and clear recovery. Apply it to survey control and status consistency review.
